content.text: Architecture Review as a Continuous Practice

A single architecture review at project inception is not sufficient for GenAI systems. Foundation model APIs evolve, usage patterns shift, cost profiles change as traffic grows, and the threat landscape moves faster than a quarterly audit can track. Enterprise GenAI platforms need a layered review cadence — different rituals at different frequencies, each scoped to the decisions that can realistically be evaluated at that interval.

This document defines five review rituals, their scope, participant set, inputs, outputs, and the automation that makes each ritual lightweight enough to sustain. The rituals nest: daily gates catch the smallest decisions, and quarterly strategy sessions address the largest ones, with each layer referencing the outputs of the layer below.

attrs.alt: Architecture review cadence pyramid — five nested rituals from daily PR gates to quarterly strategy

content.text: Why Layered Cadences Beat a Single Review Point

The cost of correcting an architectural decision escalates with time. A daily PR gate that catches a missing circuit breaker costs five minutes. A quarterly strategy session that discovers the same gap costs a sprint of rework plus regression testing. The pyramid inverts this relationship: high-frequency, low-ceremony rituals handle reversible decisions cheaply, while infrequent, high-ceremony rituals handle irreversible decisions with appropriate rigour.

Not every pull request has architectural impact. A labelling system identifies PRs that touch architectural concerns and routes them to a human reviewer in addition to the standard code review. The label is applied automatically by a CI check that scans the diff for signals.
Signals that trigger the architecture label
Changes to model ID configuration, provider selection, or fallback routing.
New external API integrations or changes to existing API contracts.
Modifications to authentication, authorisation, or secrets handling.
Changes to data retention, logging, or PII handling paths.
New vector store collections or schema changes to existing ones.
Introduction of new LLM orchestration libraries or major version bumps.

# scripts/arch_gate_check.py — runs in CI on every PR
import subprocess, sys, re

ARCH_SIGNALS = [
    r"model[_-]?(id|name|config)",      # model selection
    r"(openai|anthropic|bedrock|vertex)", # provider names
    r"vector_store|pinecone|weaviate|pgvector",
    r"OPENAI_API_KEY|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",  # secret references
    r"(pii|gdpr|data.?retention)",
    r"(langchain|llama.?index|autogen)",  # orchestration libs
    r"fallback|circuit.?breaker|retry",   # resilience patterns
]

def get_diff() -> str:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["git", "diff", "origin/main...HEAD", "--unified=0"],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    return result.stdout

def has_arch_signal(diff: str) -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
    matched = []
    for pattern in ARCH_SIGNALS:
        if re.search(pattern, diff, re.IGNORECASE):
            matched.append(pattern)
    return bool(matched), matched

if __name__ == "__main__":
    diff = get_diff()
    triggered, patterns = has_arch_signal(diff)
    if triggered:
        print(f"ARCH_GATE: architectural impact detected ({len(patterns)} signals)")
        for p in patterns:
            print(f"  - {p}")
        print("Action: apply 'arch-review' label; request review from @platform-arch")
        sys.exit(1)   # non-zero causes CI to add label via GitHub Actions step
    else:
        print("ARCH_GATE: no architectural signals detected")
        sys.exit(0)
PR review checklist for architecture-labelled PRs

## Architecture Review Checklist

### Model / provider changes
- [ ] Is the model version pinned to a date-versioned identifier (not a mutable alias)?
- [ ] Is a fallback provider configured if this is a primary model change?
- [ ] Has the cost impact been estimated (token rate × projected volume)?

### Integration / data
- [ ] Are all new external API calls authenticated and rate-limit-handled?
- [ ] Does PII flow through any new path that lacks tokenisation or masking?
- [ ] Are new vector store schemas backward-compatible?

### Security
- [ ] Are secrets injected via environment variable or secret manager (never hard-coded)?
- [ ] Does the change introduce any new injection surface (user content in prompts)?

### Observability
- [ ] Are new integration points covered by distributed traces?
- [ ] Are new error conditions surfaced to the existing alert ruleset?

The weekly design sync is a 30-minute standing meeting for engineers actively designing or building features with architectural implications. Its purpose is cross-team visibility and early blocker identification — not decision-making. Decisions are made in the ADR process; this meeting surfaces what needs a decision.

The design sync has no minutes and no action items — only blockers get a follow-up. If a topic needs a decision, it spawns an ADR. If it needs more time, it gets a dedicated spike slot. Anything else is WIP and does not need follow-up tracking.
Bi-weekly Ritual: Architecture Decision Record Review

Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) are the primary mechanism for making and recording significant technical decisions. The bi-weekly ADR review is a 60-minute structured session where pending ADRs are presented, debated, and voted on.

# ADR-042: Switch Primary Chat Model from gpt-4-turbo to gpt-4o

## Status
Proposed

## Context
gpt-4-turbo has an announced EOL of 2025-12-01. gpt-4o-2024-11-20 offers
equivalent quality at 50% lower cost and 40% lower median latency on our
eval suite (see linked benchmark). We need to migrate before EOL.

## Decision
Adopt gpt-4o-2024-11-20 as the primary chat model across all production
pipelines by 2025-11-01.

## Consequences
### Positive
- ~$8,000/month cost reduction at current volume
- 40ms median latency improvement
- Extended model lifetime (no announced EOL)

### Negative
- Requires regression testing of all prompt templates against new model
- Tool-call JSON format has minor differences — three parsers need updating

### Risks
- Q4 traffic spike may coincide with migration window; plan for staged rollout

## Alternatives considered
1. Stay on gpt-4-turbo until EOL (November): unacceptable — 90-day window too short
2. Switch to claude-sonnet: viable quality; higher cost; would require new SDK integration

## Reviewers
@alice (platform), @bob (security), @carol (product)
Quorum: at least 3 ARB members present (including the security representative if the ADR touches data handling).
Voting: Approve / Approve with conditions / Reject. Conditions must be resolved before ADR status changes to Accepted.
Any Reject blocks the ADR; the author must address objections and re-submit.
ADRs approved with conditions have a 2-week resolution window before the approval lapses.

The monthly architecture health review is a 90-minute workshop attended by platform architects, the tech lead of each product squad, and the FinOps representative. It reviews the health of the running GenAI system against three dimensions: technical debt, NFR compliance, and cost.

The debt backlog is maintained as GitHub issues tagged 'arch-debt'. At the monthly review, the team rates each item on impact (P1–P3) and effort (S/M/L), and selects items to schedule in the next sprint. Items that have sat in the backlog for more than 90 days are auto-escalated to P1 regardless of original rating.

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class NFRBaseline:
    metric: str
    target: float
    unit: str
    current: float
    trend: str        # "improving", "stable", "degrading"
    owner: str

    @property
    def status(self) -> str:
        if self.current <= self.target:
            return "GREEN"
        if self.current <= self.target * 1.1:
            return "YELLOW"   # within 10% of target
        return "RED"

    def monthly_report_line(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"{self.status:6s}  {self.metric:35s}  "
            f"target={self.target}{self.unit}  "
            f"current={self.current}{self.unit}  "
            f"trend={self.trend}  owner={self.owner}"
        )

MONTHLY_NFRS = [
    NFRBaseline("chat_p99_latency_ms",       2000, "ms",    1840, "stable",    "platform"),
    NFRBaseline("embedding_p99_latency_ms",   500, "ms",     620, "degrading", "ml-team"),
    NFRBaseline("availability_pct",          99.9, "%",     99.95,"stable",    "sre"),
    NFRBaseline("llm_cost_per_1k_tokens_usd", 0.02,"$",    0.018,"improving", "finops"),
    NFRBaseline("error_rate_pct",              1.0, "%",     0.4, "stable",    "platform"),
]

for nfr in MONTHLY_NFRS:
    print(nfr.monthly_report_line())

# Output:
# GREEN   chat_p99_latency_ms                  target=2000ms  current=1840ms  trend=stable    owner=platform
# RED     embedding_p99_latency_ms             target=500ms   current=620ms   trend=degrading owner=ml-team
# GREEN   availability_pct                     target=99.9%   current=99.95%  trend=stable    owner=sre
# GREEN   llm_cost_per_1k_tokens_usd           target=0.02$   current=0.018$  trend=improving owner=finops
# GREEN   error_rate_pct                       target=1.0%    current=0.4%    trend=stable    owner=platform
Review token spend by model, team, and feature — identify unexpected growth (>20% MoM) for investigation.
Check prompt token inflation: average prompt size creeping up is an early signal of inefficient context assembly.
Review cache hit rates for the semantic cache layer; a declining hit rate suggests prompt templates have drifted.
Compare actual vs. forecasted spend from the capacity plan; large deltas indicate the forecast model needs recalibration.

The quarterly strategy session is a half-day deep-focus block attended by principal engineers, product leadership, and the CISO or a security delegate. It operates at a longer time horizon than the other rituals and produces decisions that span multiple quarters.
Retrospective: what architecture decisions from last quarter worked, what did not?
Health review summary: escalated RED NFRs and debt backlog
Tech Radar update: new ring assignments, model version changes
Roadmap alignment: upcoming product bets that need architecture runway
Wrap-up: ADRs to open in next cycle

# docs/architecture/decisions/quarterly-log.yaml
quarters:
  - quarter: 2025-Q3
    key_decisions:
      - adr: ADR-042
        title: "Switch primary chat model to gpt-4o-2024-11-20"
        outcome: Approved
        impact: "Cost -$8k/mo; latency -40ms"

      - adr: ADR-043
        title: "Adopt LangGraph for multi-step agent orchestration"
        outcome: Approved with conditions
        condition: "Pilot on claims-processing agent before fleet rollout"
        condition_resolved: true

      - adr: ADR-044
        title: "Evaluate Weaviate as secondary vector store for tenant isolation"
        outcome: Rejected
        reason: "pgvector with row-level security meets tenant isolation requirement at lower operational cost"

    escalated_debt:
      - id: ARCH-DEBT-17
        description: "Embedding pipeline has no circuit breaker on provider timeout"
        priority: P1
        assigned_sprint: 2025-Q3-sprint-4

    radar_changes:
      - blip: langraph
        from: Trial
        to: Adopt

      - blip: gpt-4-turbo
        from: Adopt
        to: Hold

Once per year — typically aligned with fiscal year planning — a full architecture audit re-evaluates foundational decisions that are not touched by the quarterly cycle. For GenAI platforms this includes:
Re-evaluation of the primary inference provider: has the competitive landscape shifted enough to justify migration?
Security architecture review: has the threat model been updated for new attack surface introduced during the year?
Full re-score of the Tech Radar Adopt and Trial entries.
Capacity plan refresh using the full year of production traffic data.
Compliance gap analysis: have regulatory requirements changed (EU AI Act, HIPAA updates, SOC 2 controls)?
Review rituals fail when they feel like overhead rather than value. Three practices prevent cadence decay:

Automate the preparation work. Meeting inputs (NFR dashboards, cost reports, open ADR lists) should be generated by scripts and linked in the calendar invite, not assembled manually before each meeting.
Time-box ruthlessly. A 30-minute design sync that runs to 90 minutes trains engineers to skip it. End on time; defer overflow to async channels.

Publish outputs immediately. ADR decisions, radar changes, and health report summaries should be committed to the repository within 24 hours of the meeting. A decision that is not written down is not a decision.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/prep_monthly_review.sh — run the day before the monthly architecture health review

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Monthly Architecture Health Review Prep ==="

echo "\n--- NFR Status ---"
python3 scripts/nfr_report.py --format markdown > docs/architecture/reviews/nfr-status-$(date +%Y-%m).md

echo "\n--- Open Arch Debt (> 30 days) ---"
gh issue list --label arch-debt --state open --json number,title,createdAt \
  | python3 scripts/filter_stale_issues.py --days 30 \
  >> docs/architecture/reviews/nfr-status-$(date +%Y-%m).md

echo "\n--- LLM Cost Summary (last 30 days) ---"
python3 scripts/cost_summary.py --days 30 --format markdown \
  >> docs/architecture/reviews/nfr-status-$(date +%Y-%m).md

echo "\n--- Open ADRs pending review ---"
find docs/architecture/decisions -name "*.md" | xargs grep -l "Status: Proposed" \
  >> docs/architecture/reviews/nfr-status-$(date +%Y-%m).md

echo "\nPrep doc written. Share link with attendees."
Five nested review rituals span daily to quarterly frequency, each scoped to the decision reversibility appropriate for that cadence.
The daily PR architecture gate uses automated signal detection to identify arch-impact PRs and route them to a structured checklist review before merge.
The weekly design sync (30 min, no minutes) provides cross-team WIP visibility and surfaces blockers — it does not make decisions.
The bi-weekly ADR review is the primary decision-making ritual: structured proposals, quorum voting, and conditions-based approval.
The monthly architecture health review tracks NFR compliance, technical debt triage, and GenAI cost metrics against plan.
The quarterly strategy session updates the tech radar, aligns architecture with product roadmap, and opens ADRs for the next cycle.
Ritual sustainability requires automated prep, strict time-boxing, and same-day publication of outputs to the repository.
